title: new swagger tool finds tons of bugs in our code base
body:
As discussed in watercooler today, the problem is a new version of the `swagger` tool. It is not just the `default signal` problem: this is whack-a-mole, where you fix one and four more bugs pop up. This needs the attention of a swagger expert.

Reproducer:
```console
$ wget "https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/releases/download/v0.30.0/swagger_linux_amd64"
[install that somewhere in your $PATH as 'swagger', chmod it 755]
$ make swagger
```

comments.body: @umohnani8 probably worth creating a card for this one.  I assume (but don't know for sure) that fixing these issues will improve the REST API docs.
Probably worth checking how many of these issues were fixed in #15614

Note I just looked at https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13342 and tried swagger v0.30.0. The static binary from this release page seems to create a completely incorrect swagger.yaml. There are many fields missing for unknown reasons. When I build the same version from upstream locally it works as expected.

Also could you list the warnings? Every time I used swagger in the past it always listed many warnings, I don't think this is new.

>Also could you list the warnings?

My recollection is that they were errors, not warnings, and the tool would stop processing after each error. Fix one, rerun, stop on the next error, repeat. I'm not swagger-savvy enough to test this, and I'm especially not able to fix swagger bugs.

I only see warnings with that version.
But more importantly this version seems to miss a ton of fields in the final swagger.yaml. If I compile the upstream version it seems to work as expected.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> Looks like nobody cares.

I do care but it's more a prioritization issue for me.  There's an infinite stream of tasks on my pipeline but I wished we can tackle this issue.
